shorts.hook: Your money story was set by age 7
shorts.content: Your relationship with money was shaped before you could even tie your shoes. By age 7, through interactions with parents, peers, and trusted adults, you've already developed invisible scripts that drive your financial behavior for life. The beliefs holding you back? They might not even be yours.
shorts.title: Why Your Money Mindset Was Set Before Age 7

shorts.hook: The investing gap costs more than wage gap
shorts.content: The investing gap is costing women far more than the wage gap, and the only way to close it is to stop waiting and start investing. Compound interest doubles your money roughly every 10 years. The longer you wait, the more you're losing in potential wealth.
shorts.title: This Gap Costs Women More Than the Wage Gap

shorts.content: Kids who learn about investing before 16 are 3 times more likely to be confident investors as adults. Financial agency begins with teaching children to make real money decisions, not just theoretical lessons about saving. Start early, impact lasts a lifetime.
shorts.title: Why Kids Must Learn Investing Before Age 16

shorts.content: Women receive only 2-3% of all available capital. This is exactly why it's crucial for women to start investing in women-led businesses and startups. We need to change who controls the money to change who gets funded. The power is in our hands.
shorts.title: The Shocking Truth About Women and Capital

titleOptions.curiosityGap: What Your 7-Year-Old Self Decided About Money (It's Costing You)
titleOptions.boldStatement: The Money Story That's Costing Women Millions
titleOptions.numberBased: 4 Money Stories That Control Your Financial Future

description:
Your money story was shaped before you could tie your shoes—and it's costing women more than the wage gap ever could. 💰

Financial expert Stephanie Guttman reveals how to identify and rewrite the invisible scripts controlling your financial decisions.

⏰ TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 Introduction
2:15 How your money story forms by age 7
8:30 The 4 core money stories explained
15:45 Why the investing gap costs more than wage gap
22:10 ETFs made simple for beginners
28:20 Teaching kids about money and investing
35:15 Women and access to capital
42:00 Action steps to rewrite your money story

🔑 KEY TAKEAWAYS:
• Your money story forms by age 7 through family interactions
• Only 1 of 4 money stories leads to positive financial outcomes
• Compound interest doubles money every 10 years
• ETFs make investing accessible without beating the market
• Kids learning investing before 16 are 3x more confident investors
• Women receive only 2-3% of available capital

communityPosts.content:
Which money story resonates most with you? 💰

🙈 AVOIDANCE: "Money is the root of all evil" - avoiding financial decisions
💸 MONEY WORSHIP: "Money will solve all my problems" - believing money equals happiness
👑 STATUS: "My self-worth = my net worth" - using money for image
🔍 VIGILANCE: "Money is scarce and must be managed carefully" - the only one linked to positive outcomes

Your money story was likely formed by age 7 through family interactions. The good news? You can rewrite it at any age! ✍️

Watch my latest episode with financial expert @huntresswealth to learn how → link in bio

"The investing gap is costing women far more than the wage gap, and the only way to close it is to stop waiting and start investing." - @huntresswealth 💡

This hit me hard during our conversation. While we focus on earning equal pay, we're missing the bigger wealth-building opportunity.

Compound interest doubles your money roughly every 10 years. The longer you wait to invest, the more potential wealth you're leaving on the table. 📈

ETFs make it simple - you can invest in hundreds of stocks with a single purchase. No need to beat the market or pick individual winners.

Ready to close your investing gap? Start today, even if it's small. Your future self will thank you. 💪

#WomenInvesting #InvestingGap #CompoundInterest #WealthBuilding
